genoprobe

  • Quick start

Command reference

  • targets — Define probe-design target regions
  • probes — Generate probe candidates
  • screen — Off-target screening
  • panels — Assemble final probe panels
  • index — Build a Fulgor index

Background

  • Profiles
  • Thermodynamics

Developer reference

  • Python API reference
genoprobe
  • Installation
  • View page source

Installation

Requirements

  • Python ≥ 3.10

  • pyfaidx ≥ 0.8

  • rich ≥ 13.7

  • typer ≥ 0.12

Installing from PyPI

pip install genoprobe

Installing from source

git clone https://github.com/anttonalberdi/genoprobe.git
cd genoprobe
pip install -e .

Optional dependencies

Thermodynamic backend (primer3)

The default Tm calculation uses the built-in nearest-neighbour model (no extra dependencies). For higher-accuracy calculations, install the primer3-py backend:

pip install "genoprobe[thermo]"

Then pass --thermo-backend primer3 to the probes command.

Conda environment

A conda environment.yml is provided for a fully reproducible environment:

conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate genoprobe

Optional: Fulgor index

The screen command can use a Fulgor coloured compacted de Bruijn graph index for fast, genome-wide k-mer lookups. Fulgor must be installed separately and available on PATH. The index command builds the index; see Building a Fulgor index.

Verifying the installation

genoprobe --version

You should see output such as genoprobe 0.1.0.
